title: Location
summary: 9609 A Level — location factors, domestic vs overseas, cost–market trade-offs.
learningObjectives: Analyse cost vs market proximity trade-offs.
Evaluate location recommendations for case firms.

simpleExplanation.title: Where to operate
simpleExplanation.summary: Location decisions balance labour costs and skills, access to materials and transport, government incentives, and whether to sit near customers or near resources.
steps: Labour costs, availability, and skill levels.
Raw material supply and transport infrastructure.
Government grants, tax breaks, and regulations.

flashcards.back: Wage rates, productivity, unions, training pools — links to 4.1.3.
Heavy/bulky inputs favour location near suppliers.
Perishable or bulky products favour location near customers.
Free trade zones, subsidies — PESTLE political/economic (6.1).
Supply chain disruption, quality control, exchange rates.
Firms cluster in same area — shared skills and suppliers.
Long transport routes increase carbon footprint (4.1.2).
Retail/hotels need footfall and accessibility not raw materials.

sections.content: **Location** (9.1.1) opens **A Level Operations (Topic 9)**. Weight **factors for the case country** — don't list generic factors without application.

sections.question: Electric vehicle battery maker supplies European car plants. Compare locating factory in (A) Germany near customers or (B) Eastern Europe with lower wages and government grants.
sections.solution:
**Germany (near market):** **Lower transport cost** to OEMs, **skilled engineering labour**, **strong IP protection** — but **high wages** and **energy costs**.

**Eastern Europe:** **Lower labour**, **grants**, growing **supplier cluster** — but **longer delivery** to some plants, **training investment** needed.

**Decision drivers:** **Just-in-time contracts** (4.2.2) favour **proximity**; **grant size** and **labour cost gap** favour **East**.

**Recommendation:** **Eastern Europe** if logistics reliable; keep **R&D in Germany** — split functions.
